lines.en_lit: But there is this higher standard, that whereas from the Sanctuary they remove both during its own sanctification, and during the sanctification of the innermost but from between the Entrance Hall and the altar, they remove only during the sanctification of the Sanctuary. Rava said: one category of removal.

The Master said they similarly remove during the presentations of the bull of the anointed priest; of the bull for an unwitting sin; and of the goats of idol worship. From where do we Rabbi Pedat said: atonement atonement from Yom Kippur.
Rav Aḥa bar Ahava said: Learn from this higher standards by Torah law, and learned them

As if it could enter your mind by rabbinic law, what is different between the Entrance Hall and the altar Perhaps happen to enter they remove also from the entire Temple courtyard, perhaps they happen to enter.
between the Entrance Hall and the altar, Since there is nothing that separates it is not conspicuous, Temple courtyard, since there is the outer altar that separates is conspicuous,

Rava said: Learn from this The sanctity of the Entrance Hall Sanctuary is one matter, For if it could enter your mind these have two of sanctity, Entrance Hall is itself decree. But will we arise and issue decree to decree
No, the Entrance Hall and the between the Entrance Hall and the altar one sanctity. the Sanctuary and the Entrance Hall have two sanctities.
On every day, would scoop up of silver and pour into of gold. What is the reason the Torah spared the money of the Jewish people.
But this day, scoops up with of gold, and with he would bring into What is the reason Due to the weakness High Priest.
On every day, of four kav It was taught a kav of coals became scattered, and he swept them into the canal
was taught one A kav And it was taught in another Two kav Granted, this teaches that kav the Rabbis, But that teaches two kav with whose It is not the Rabbis and not Rabbi Yosei.
Rav Ḥisda said: It is Rabbi Yishmael, son of Rabbi Yoḥanan ben Beroka, as it was taught Rabbi Yishmael, son of Rabbi Yoḥanan ben Beroka, says: With of two kav he would bring into
Rav Ashi said: You can even say Rabbi Yosei, and this is what he is saying: On every day, scooped with of a desert se’a, which and he poured into a coal pan of three Jerusalem kav.

On every day, was heavy, but this day light. It was taught On every day its side was thick but this day soft On every day was short but this day it was long. What is the reason? So that the arm of the High Priest could assist him
taught On every day it did not have a ring, but this day it has a ring the statement of the son of the Deputy.

On every day, it was greenish gold Rav Ḥisda said: There are seven of gold Gold, and good gold, and gold of Ophir and glistering gold and shaḥut gold and closed gold and parvayim gold gold and good gold, as it is written “And the gold of that land is good” Gold of Ophir that comes from Ophir. Glistering [mufaz] gold
